articles.title: What People in China Say of Trump
articles.summary: Residents in four Chinese cities described a mixture of amusement and anger, blaming U.S. tensions for a slowing economy and rising fuel prices.

articles.title: An LA-area mayor acted as an agent for China. Experts say it's part of a pattern
articles.summary: Eileen Wang, now the former mayor of the City of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty to one felony charge that she acted as an illegal foreign agent of China.

articles.title: Trump tries to make deals in China. And, Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed leader
articles.summary: President Trump is vying to make deals with China during his visit with President Xi Jinping. And, the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next leader of the Federal Reserve.

articles.title: Richard Haass talks about accounts from U.S. and China on Trump-Xi meeting
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with veteran diplomat Richard Haass about the differences in Chinese and U.S. readouts from President Trump's meeting with China's leader Xi Jinping.

articles.title: China's leader warns Trump that differences over Taiwan could lead to a clash
articles.summary: At the summit, the Chinese leader placed Taiwan, a key point of friction between the U.S. and China, at the center, calling it "the most important issue" between the two countries.

articles.title: President Trump's first day in China
articles.summary: President Trump met with Chinese President Xi Jingping {shee-jihn-ping} during his first day in the country. A look at what happened at the high-stakes summit.

articles.title: China’s Growing Leverage Over the U.S., and How Oil Companies Hit the Jackpot
articles.summary: Plus, a surprise reversal in the Murdaugh murder case.

articles.title: How Asia is reacting to Trump's summit in China
articles.summary: President Trump is in Beijing for his big summit with Chinese leaders. How Asia is reacting to the summit.

articles.title: Consumers Spent More in April Despite High Gas Prices
articles.summary: Retail sales rose 0.5 percent despite higher prices for gas, food and other goods. But there are signs consumers are under some strain.

articles.title: Bo French Challenges Texas Oil Regulator in GOP Runoff
articles.summary: Bo French, a hard-liner known for wild social media posts, is seeking a bigger megaphone. Big oil companies, and top officials like Greg Abbott, want to stop him.

articles.title: A Philippine senator wanted by the International Criminal Court flees from Senate
articles.summary: A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.

articles.title: Inside Jack Schlossberg’s Chaotic Campaign to Revive Camelot
articles.summary: Erratic behavior and staff turnover have colored Mr. Schlossberg’s bid for a House seat in New York, raising questions about his readiness for office.

articles.title: A Fact Check of Trump’s Claims on Inflation and Gas Prices in Iran War
articles.summary: He has minimized soaring gas prices, rising inflation and the American economy’s need for the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: A brain-controlled system may help listeners with hearing loss cut through the noise
articles.summary: A hearing system that monitors brain waves could help people with hearing loss communicate in noisy environments.

articles.title: The New Yorker's Evan Osnos shares his thoughts on the Trump-Xi summit
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with the New Yorker's Evan Osnos about his impressions of the Trump-Xi summit.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon meet in D.C. again for peace talks
articles.summary: Representatives for Israel and Lebanon meet in Washington, D.C., for a second time to end the war, as their ceasefire frays.

articles.title: Why the Migrant Child Crisis Is Roiling the California Governor Race
articles.summary: The Times broke the story that has become a dominant line of attack against Xavier Becerra, the Democratic front-runner. Here are five things to know about it.

articles.title: Voters are caught in the middle as the redistricting battle intensifies
articles.summary: Much of the focus of the ongoing redistricting war has been on which political party will come out on top. But it's voters who will pay a cost, say voting experts and voting rights advocates.

articles.title: Ex-DOJ official goes public with blistering criticism of his former bosses
articles.summary: Until recently, Jonathan Gross was a Trump political appointee at the Department of Justice and worked on its "Weaponization Working Group." He has now become a vocal critic of the department.

articles.title: The MAHA movement is coming to school cafeterias. Here's what that means for kids
articles.summary: U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.

articles.title: Trump’s Remark on Americans’ Finances and Iran War Points to GOP Midterm Challenges
articles.summary: Asked if he was motivated by Americans’ financial woes to make a deal to end the war with Iran, he responded, “Not even a little bit.”

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
